title: {mnirs} Agent Reference
text: Order rules: - resample → replace: window replacement needs regular time_channel - replace → filter: "smooth_spline" / "butterworth" error internal NA (unless na.rm = TRUE) - resample → filter("smooth_spline"): errors duplicated time values - resample → extract_intervals(event_groups = "ensemble"): ensemble needs regular samples - extract_intervals → analyse_kinetics multiple intervals
code: read_mnirs()   └─ resample_mnirs()                  # regularise time grid       └─ replace_mnirs()               # clean invalid/outliers/NA           └── filter_mnirs()           # smooth               ├── shift_mnirs()        # optional: shift baseline               ├── rescale_mnirs()      # optional: normalise range               └── extract_intervals()                 └── analyse_kinetics()                   └── plot() / print()

title: {mnirs} Agent Reference
code: read_mnirs(     file_path,     nirs_channels = NULL,   # char vec; named = rename: c(new = "old")     time_channel  = NULL,   # char(1); named = rename: c(time = "Timestamp")     event_channel = NULL,   # char(1); optional; named = rename     sample_rate   = NULL,   # numeric(1) Hz; estimated if NULL     add_timestamp = FALSE,  # add POSIXct "timestamp" column     zero_time     = FALSE,  # rebase time[1] to 0     keep_all      = FALSE,  # keep all columns, otherwise only specified channels     verbose       = TRUE )  example_mnirs(file = NULL) ## files: "moxy_ramp", "train.red", "artinis", "portamon", "vo2master" ## blank → returns all names; partial matching; pass to read_mnirs(file_path = ...)  create_mnirs_data(data, ...)  # low-level constructor; wraps df as "mnirs"

title: {mnirs} Agent Reference
text: method = "none": new samples → NA Non-numeric cols always use "locf" resample_rate = sample_rate: regularise , rate change
code: resample_mnirs(     data,     time_channel  = NULL,     sample_rate   = NULL,         # source Hz; estimated if NULL     resample_rate = sample_rate,  # target Hz; default = source     method = c("none", "locf", "linear"),     verbose = TRUE )

title: {mnirs} Agent Reference
text: Processing order: invalid → outliers → missing (NA). Vector-level functions: replace_invalid() / replace_outliers() default method = "median". replace_mnirs() / replace_missing() default "linear".
code: replace_mnirs(     data,     nirs_channels  = NULL,     time_channel   = NULL,     invalid_values = NULL,   # numeric vec; exact values to replace     invalid_above  = NULL,   # numeric(1); replace x > threshold     invalid_below  = NULL,   # numeric(1); replace x < threshold     outlier_cutoff = NULL,   # numeric(1); Hampel MAD multiplier; NULL = skip     width          = NULL,   # integer; rolling window in samples     span           = NULL,   # numeric; rolling window in time units     method = c("linear", "median", "locf", "none"),     verbose = TRUE ) replace_invalid(x, t, invalid_values, invalid_above, invalid_below,                 width, span, method = c("median", "none"), ...) replace_outliers(x, t, outlier_cutoff = 3, width, span,                  method = c("median", "none"), ...) replace_missing(x, t, width, span,                 method = c("linear", "median", "locf"), ...)

title: {mnirs} Agent Reference
text: Aliases: Method-specific arguments: "smooth_spline" (stats::smooth.spline()): spar (NULL = GCV auto); errors NA/duplicated time. "butterworth" (requires signal): order (default 2L), W/fc (normalised Hz), type ("low", "high", "stop", "pass"), edges ("rev", "rep1", "none"); errors NA. "moving_average": width (samples) span (time units); partial (default FALSE). Vector-level:
code: filter_mnirs(     data,     nirs_channels = NULL,     time_channel  = NULL,     method = c("smooth_spline", "butterworth", "moving_average"),     na.rm   = FALSE,       # bypass NAs     verbose = TRUE,     ... ) filter_ma(x, t, width, span, partial = FALSE, na.rm = FALSE, ...) filter_moving_average()    # alias for filter_ma() filter_butter(x, order = 2L, W, type = "low", edges = "rev", na.rm = FALSE)

title: {mnirs} Agent Reference
text: nirs_channels list semantics (functions): Default retrieved metadata: list(c("", "B")).
code: shift_mnirs(     data,     nirs_channels = list(),  # list()/NULL → auto from metadata     time_channel  = NULL,     to       = NULL,         # numeric(1); target level (overrides `by`)     by       = NULL,         # numeric(1); shift amount     width    = NULL,         # integer; window in samples     span     = NULL,         # numeric; window in time units     position = c("min", "max", "first"),     verbose  = TRUE )  rescale_mnirs(     data,     nirs_channels = list(),     range,                   # numeric(2): c(min, max)     verbose = TRUE )

title: {mnirs} Agent Reference
text: Returns named list "mnirs" dfs. Raw coercion: numeric → by_time(); character → by_label(); explicit integer → by_lap() start = by_lap(), end = NULL: full lap range start = by_lap(), end = by_lap(): first start sample → last end sample span = c(, ) recycled boundaries; negative = start, positive = end span = list() recycled intervals event_groups = "distinct": one df per event event_groups = "ensemble": single ensemble-averaged df (requires regular time grid) event_groups = list(c(1,2), c(3,4)): one ensemble df per group Names: "ensemble" "interval_1", "interval_2", …
code: extract_intervals(     data,     nirs_channels = NULL,     time_channel  = NULL,     event_channel = NULL,     sample_rate   = NULL,     start         = NULL,   # by_time(numeric), by_label(character), by_lap(integer), by_sample(numericfs)     end           = NULL,   # same; NULL = use span around start     span          = list(c(-60, 60)),  # c(before, after) per interval     event_groups  = c("distinct", "ensemble"),     zero_time     = FALSE,  # rebase time[1] to 0 per interval     verbose       = TRUE )

title: {mnirs} Agent Reference
code: analyse_kinetics(     data,           # "mnirs" df | named list of "mnirs" dfs | {dplyr} grouped df     nirs_channels  = NULL,     time_channel   = NULL,     method = c("half_response_time", "peak_slope", "monoexponential", "sigmoidal"),     direction = c("auto", "positive", "negative"),     end_fit_span   = Inf,    # truncate fit after extreme; Inf = global extreme     channel_args   = list(), # per nirs_channel argument overrides     verbose        = TRUE,     ... )

title: {mnirs} Agent Reference
text: Aliases: Input: single df → "interval_1"; named list → separate; grouped df → split group. Per-method args ...: "half_response_time": t0 (default 0), fraction (default 0.5; 0.632 ≈ MRT) "peak_slope": width span (one required); align ("centre", "left", "right"); partial (default FALSE) "monoexponential": time_delay (default TRUE; 4-param → 3-param fallback); accepts stats::nls() args Per-channel overrides: Names must match nirs_channels exactly; unrecognised names silently ignored. Coefficients: "half_response_time": interval, nirs_channels, time_channel, , B, response_time, response_value, fitted, idx "peak_slope": interval, nirs_channels, time_channel, slope, intercept, fitted, <time_channel>, idx "monoexponential": interval, nirs_channels, time_channel, , B, tau, k, TD, MRT, HRT, tau_fitted, MRT_fitted, HRT_fitted Diagnostics: n_obs, r2, adj_r2, pseudo_r2, rmse, snr, cv_rmse Vector-level:
code: analyse_kinetics(data, nirs_channels = c(hhb, smo2), method = "peak_slope",     span = 5, channel_args = list(smo2 = list(span = 10), hhb = list(direction = "negative"))) monoexponential(t, A, B, tau, TD) ## 3-parameter equation: `A + (B - A) * (1 - exp(-t / tau))` ## 4-parameter equation: `ifelse(t <= TD, A, A + (B - A) * (1 - exp(-(t - TD) / tau)))`  nls(x ~ SS_monoexp3(t, A, B, tau), data = df) nls(x ~ SS_monoexp4(t, A, B, tau, TD), data = df)  peak_slope(x, t = seq_along(x), width = NULL, span = NULL,     align = c("centre", "left", "right"),     direction = c("auto", "positive", "negative"),     partial = FALSE, na.rm = FALSE, verbose = TRUE) ## returns: slope, intercept, y, t, idx, fitted, window_idx, model  response_time(x, t = seq_along(x), t0 = 0, fraction = 0.5,     end_fit_span = Inf, direction = c("auto", "positive", "negative"),     verbose = TRUE) ## returns: A, B, response_time, response_value, fitted, ##          baseline_idx, response_idx, extreme_idx

title: Reading and Cleaning Data with {mnirs}
text: Modern wearable muscle near-infrared spectroscopy (mNIRS) devices make easier ever monitor local muscle oxygenation dynamic activities. real challenge comes deciding clean, filter, process, eventually interpret data. mnirs package aims provide standardised, reproducible methods reading, processing, analysing mNIRS data. goal help practitioners detect meaningful signal noise, improve confidence interpreting applying information clients work . vignette demonstrate : 📂 Read data files exported commercial wearable NIRS devices, import NIRS channels standard data frame format metadata, ready processing. 📊 Plot visualise data frames class "mnirs". 🔍 Retrieve metadata stored data frames class "mnirs" avoid repetitively specifying channels process. ⏱️ Resample data higher lower sample rate, correct irregular sampling periods, match frequency data sources synchronisation. 🧹 Detect replace local outliers, invalid values, interpolate across missing data. 📈️ Apply digital filtering optimise signal--noise ratio responses observed data. ⚖️ Shift rescale across multiple NIRS channels, normalise signal dynamic range preserving absolute relative scaling muscle sites NIRS channels. 🔀 Demonstrate complete data wrangling process using pipe-friendly functions. 🧮 Detect extract intervals analysis. mnirs designed process NIRS data, can used read, clean, process time series data require many processing steps. Enjoy!

code: # pak::pak("jemarnold/mnirs") ## install development version library(ggplot2)   ## for plotting library(patchwork) ## for plotting library(mnirs) nirs_channels = c(     renamed1 = "original_name1",      renamed2 = "original_name2" ) ## {mnirs} includes sample files from a few NIRS devices example_mnirs() #> [1] "artinis_intervals.xlsx"  "moxy_intervals.csv"      #> [3] "moxy_ramp.xlsx"          "portamon-oxcap.xlsx"     #> [5] "train.red_intervals.csv"  ## partial matching will error if matches multiple try(example_mnirs("moxy")) #> Error in example_mnirs("moxy") : ✖ Multiple files match "moxy": #> ℹ Matching files: "moxy_intervals.csv" and "moxy_ramp.xlsx"  data_raw <- read_mnirs(     file_path = example_mnirs("moxy_ramp"), ## call an example data file     nirs_channels = c(         smo2_left = "SmO2 Live",    ## identify and rename channels         smo2_right = "SmO2 Live(2)"     ),     time_channel = c(time = "hh:mm:ss"), ## date-time format will be converted to numeric     event_channel = NULL,           ## leave blank if unused     sample_rate = NULL,             ## if blank, will be estimated from time_channel     add_timestamp = FALSE,          ## omit a date-time timestamp column     zero_time = TRUE,               ## recalculate time values from zero     keep_all = FALSE,               ## return only the specified data channels     verbose = TRUE                  ## show warnings & messages ) #> ! Estimated `sample_rate` = 2 Hz. #> ℹ Define `sample_rate` explicitly to override. #> Warning: ! Duplicate or irregular `time_channel` samples detected. #> ℹ Investigate at `time` = 211.99 and 1184. #> ℹ Re-sample with `mnirs::resample_mnirs()`.  ## Note the above info message that sample_rate was estimated correctly at 2 Hz ☝ ## ignore the warnings about irregular sampling for now, we will resample later  data_raw #> # A tibble: 2,203 × 3 #>     time smo2_left smo2_right #>    <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl> #>  1 0            54         68 #>  2 0.400        54         68 #>  3 0.960        54         68 #>  4 1.51         54         66 #>  5 2.06         54         66 #>  6 2.61         54         66 #>  7 3.16         54         66 #>  8 3.71         57         67 #>  9 4.26         57         67 #> 10 4.81         57         67 #> # ℹ 2,193 more rows

text: mNIRS devices may sample rates anywhere 0.5 100+ Hz. Higher sample rates gives greater precision. However, issues can arise irregular sampling time samples occur inconsistent frequencies, repeated, lost. text: NIRS signal cleaned filtered, ready processing analysis. mnirs development include functionality processing discrete intervals events, e.g. reoxygenation kinetics, slope calculations post-occlusion microvascular responsiveness, critical oxygenation breakpoints. Often need locate extract smaller intervals NIRS recording processing analysis. example, want extract last three minutes repeated exercise trials. may marked event labels incremental lap numbers specified event_channel, may simply manually specify event markers time_channel value row number data frame. See extract_intervals() details. data function takes data frame, applies processing channels specified, returns list processed data frames. mnirs metadata passed function. nirs_channels returning list “distinct” intervals (see event_groups ), nirs_channels specified, channels processed. “ensemble”-averaging, nirs_channels specified providing list column names (e.g. list(c(, B), c())), list item specifies channels ensemble-averaged within respective group (ensemble-groups specified event_groups ), order returned. default mnirs metadata ensemble-average nirs_channels. time_channel, event_channel, & sample_rate data contain mnirs metadata, detected automatically, can specified explicitly. start & end Interval boundaries specified using helper functions: by_time() time_channel values; by_sample() sample indices (row numbers); by_label() event_channel labels; by_lap() event_channel lap integers. Provide start end define precise intervals, provide start alone span extract windows around events. Multiple values can passed extract multiple intervals. event_groups Events can extracted returned list "distinct" intervals, "ensemble"-averaged single data frame. Custom grouping structure ensemble-averaging can specified event number, order appearance within original data. e.g. event_groups = list(c(1, 2), c(3, 4)) return list two intervals, ensemble-averaged respective events, sequential order original data. span start (end) provided, span specifies time window units time_channel c(, ), positive values indicate time event negative values indicate time . start end provided, span shifts boundaries additively: span[1] adjusts starts, span[2] adjusts ends. list unique span vectors can specified interval, otherwise single span vector recycled intervals. zero_time FALSE default; time_channel values within returned interval likely start non-zero value, zero_time = TRUE re-calculate time starting zero specified event, start time (however event specified) become 0. ensemble-averaging across intervals, time always re-calculated 0, since time values lost meaning.   ensemble-averaged kinetics capture representative ‘average’ response two interval deoxygenation events. Ensemble-averaging can help mitigate data dropouts quality issues measurement error biological variability. example, ensemble-averaging common evaluating systemic VO2 kinetics, responses can highly variable trial trial.
code: ## return each interval independently with `event_groups = "distinct"` distinct <- extract_intervals(     nirs_data,                  ## channels blank for "distinct" grouping     start = by_time(348, 1064), ## manually identified interval start times     end = by_time(458, 1174),   ## interval end time (start + 150 sec)     event_groups = "distinct",  ## return a list of data frames for each (2) event     span = c(0, 0),             ## no boundary modification     zero_time = FALSE           ## return original time values )  plot(distinct[[1L]], time_labels = TRUE) + plot(distinct[[2L]], time_labels = TRUE) ## ensemble average both intervals with `event_groups = "ensemble"` ensemble <- extract_intervals(     nirs_data,                  ## channels recycled to all intervals by default     nirs_channels = c(smo2_left, smo2_right),     start = by_time(368, 1084), ## alternatively specify start times + span     event_groups = "ensemble",  ## ensemble-average across two intervals     span = c(-20, 90),          ## span recycled to all intervals by default     zero_time = TRUE            ## re-calculate common time to start from `0` )  plot(ensemble[[1L]], time_labels = TRUE) +      geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted")

code: library(ggplot2) ## for plotting library(patchwork) ## for plotting library(mnirs)  ## {mnirs} includes sample files from a few mNIRS devices example_mnirs() #> [1] "artinis_intervals.xlsx"  "moxy_intervals.csv"      #> [3] "moxy_ramp.xlsx"          "portamon-oxcap.xlsx"     #> [5] "train.red_intervals.csv"  ## rename channels in the format `renamed = "original_name"` ## where "original_name1" should match the file column name exactly data_raw <- read_mnirs(     file_path = example_mnirs("moxy_ramp"), ## call an example data file     nirs_channels = c(         smo2_left = "SmO2 Live",    ## identify and rename channels         smo2_right = "SmO2 Live(2)"     ),     time_channel = c(time = "hh:mm:ss"), ## date-time format will be converted to numeric     event_channel = NULL,           ## leave blank if unused     sample_rate = NULL,             ## if blank, will be estimated from time_channel     add_timestamp = FALSE,          ## omit a date-time timestamp column     zero_time = TRUE,               ## recalculate time values from zero     keep_all = FALSE,               ## return only the specified data channels     verbose = TRUE                  ## show warnings & messages ) #> ! Estimated `sample_rate` = 2 Hz. #> ℹ Define `sample_rate` explicitly to override. #> Warning: ! Duplicate or irregular `time_channel` samples detected. #> ℹ Investigate at `time` = 211.99 and 1184. #> ℹ Re-sample with `mnirs::resample_mnirs()`.  ## Note the above info message that sample_rate was estimated correctly at 2 Hz 👆 ## ignore the warnings about irregular sampling for now, we will resample later  data_raw #> # A tibble: 2,203 × 3 #>     time smo2_left smo2_right #>    <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl> #>  1 0            54         68 #>  2 0.400        54         68 #>  3 0.960        54         68 #>  4 1.51         54         66 #>  5 2.06         54         66 #>  6 2.61         54         66 #>  7 3.16         54         66 #>  8 3.71         57         67 #>  9 4.26         57         67 #> 10 4.81         57         67 #> # ℹ 2,193 more rows  ## note the `time_labels` plot argument to display time values as `h:mm:ss` plot(     data_raw,     time_labels = TRUE,     n.breaks = 5,     na.omit = FALSE )

what: replace_mnirs: Replace local outliers, invalid values, and missing values
title: Muscle Near-Infrared Spectroscopy Processing and Analysis
code: data_cleaned <- replace_mnirs(     data_resampled,     ## blank channels will be retrieved from metadata     invalid_values = 0, ## known invalid values in the data     invalid_above = 90, ## remove data spikes above 90     outlier_cutoff = 3, ## recommended default value     width = 7,          ## window to detect and replace outliers/missing values     method = "linear"   ## linear interpolation over `NA`s )  plot(data_cleaned, time_labels = TRUE)

title: Muscle Near-Infrared Spectroscopy Processing and Analysis
code: data_filtered <- filter_mnirs(     data_cleaned,           ## blank channels will be retrieved from metadata     method = "butterworth", ## Butterworth digital filter is a common choice     order = 2,              ## filter order number     W = 0.02,               ## filter fractional critical frequency `[0, 1]`     type = "low",           ## specify a "low-pass" filter     na.rm = TRUE            ## explicitly ignore NAs )  ## we will add the non-filtered data back to the plot to compare plot(data_filtered, time_labels = TRUE) +     geom_line(         data = data_cleaned,          aes(y = smo2_left, colour = "smo2_left"), alpha = 0.4     ) +     geom_line(         data = data_cleaned,          aes(y = smo2_right, colour = "smo2_right"), alpha = 0.4     )

what: shift_mnirs() & rescale_mnirs(): Shift and rescale data
title: Muscle Near-Infrared Spectroscopy Processing and Analysis
code: data_shifted <- shift_mnirs(     data_filtered,     ## un-grouped nirs channels to shift separately      nirs_channels = list(smo2_left, smo2_right),      to = 0,            ## NIRS values will be shifted to zero     span = 120,        ## shift the *first* 120 sec of data to zero     position = "first" )  plot(data_shifted, time_labels = TRUE) +     geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") data_rescaled <- rescale_mnirs(     data_filtered,    ## un-grouped nirs channels to rescale separately      nirs_channels = list(smo2_left, smo2_right),      range = c(0, 100) ## rescale to a 0-100% functional exercise range )  plot(data_rescaled, time_labels = TRUE) +     geom_hline(yintercept = c(0, 100), linetype = "dotted")

title: Muscle Near-Infrared Spectroscopy Processing and Analysis
code: ## global option to silence info & warning messages options(mnirs.verbose = FALSE)  nirs_data <- read_mnirs(     example_mnirs("train.red"),     nirs_channels = c(         smo2_left = "SmO2 unfiltered",         smo2_right = "SmO2 unfiltered"     ),     time_channel = c(time = "Timestamp (seconds passed)"),     zero_time = TRUE ) |>     resample_mnirs(method = "linear") |> ## default settings will resample to the same `sample_rate`     replace_mnirs(         invalid_above = 73,         outlier_cutoff = 3,         span = 7     ) |>     filter_mnirs(         method = "butterworth",         order = 2,         W = 0.02,         na.rm = TRUE     ) |>     shift_mnirs(         nirs_channels = list(smo2_left, smo2_right), ## 👈 channels grouped separately         to = 0,         span = 60,         position = "first"     ) |>     rescale_mnirs(         nirs_channels = list(c(smo2_left, smo2_right)), ## 👈 channels grouped together         range = c(0, 100)     )  plot(nirs_data, time_labels = TRUE)

what: extract_intervals(): detect events and extract intervals
title: Muscle Near-Infrared Spectroscopy Processing and Analysis
code: ## return each interval independently with `event_groups = "distinct"` distinct <- extract_intervals(     nirs_data,                  ## channels blank for "distinct" grouping     start = by_time(348, 1064), ## manually identified interval start times     end = by_time(458, 1174),   ## interval end time (start + 150 sec)     event_groups = "distinct",  ## return a list of data frames for each (2) event     span = c(0, 0),             ## no boundary modification     zero_time = FALSE           ## return original time values )  plot(distinct[[1L]], time_labels = TRUE) + plot(distinct[[2L]], time_labels = TRUE) ## ensemble average both intervals with `event_groups = "ensemble"` ensemble <- extract_intervals(     nirs_data,                  ## channels recycled to all intervals by default     nirs_channels = c(smo2_left, smo2_right),     start = by_time(368, 1084), ## alternatively specify start times + span     event_groups = "ensemble",  ## ensemble-average across two intervals     span = c(-20, 90),          ## span recycled to all intervals by default     zero_time = TRUE            ## re-calculate common time to start from `0` )  plot(ensemble[[1L]], time_labels = TRUE) +      geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted")

title: Computes rolling local values — compute_local_windows
text: compute_local_windows(): Compute list rolling window indices along time variable t. compute_local_fun(): Compute rolling function along x list rolling sample windows. compute_outliers(): Computes vector local medians logicals indicating outliers x within list rolling sample windows window_idx. compute_valid_neighbours(): Compute list rolling window indices along x either side NAs.

title: Computes rolling local values — compute_local_windows
code: compute_local_windows(   t,   idx = seq_along(t),   width = NULL,   span = NULL,   align = c("centre", "left", "right") )  compute_local_fun(x, window_idx, fn, ...)  compute_outliers(x, window_idx, outlier_cutoff)  compute_valid_neighbours(   x,   t = seq_along(x),   width = NULL,   span = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Computes rolling local values — compute_local_windows
text: t optional numeric vector predictor variable (e.g. time). Default seq_along(x). idx numeric vector indices t calculate local windows. indices t default, can used calculate known indices, invalid values x. width integer defining local window number samples around idx perform operation, according align. span numeric value defining local window timespan around idx perform operation, according align. units time_channel t. align Window alignment "centre"/"center" (default), "left", "right". "left" forward looking, "right" backward looking current sample. x numeric vector response variable. window_idx list shorter length x numeric vectors sample indices local rolling windows. fn function pass local rolling calculation. ... Additional arguments. outlier_cutoff numeric value local outlier threshold, number standard deviations local median. Default NULL replace outliers. Lower values sensitive flag outliers; higher values conservative. outlier_cutoff = 3 Pearson's 3 sigma edit rule. outlier_cutoff = 2 approximates Tukey-style 1.5×IQR rule. outlier_cutoff = 0 Tukey's median filter. verbose Logical. Default TRUE. display silence (FALSE) warnings information messages helpful troubleshooting. global default can set via options(mnirs.verbose = FALSE).

title: Computes rolling local values — compute_local_windows
text: compute_local_windows(): list length idx shorter length t numeric vectors sample indices length width samples span units time t. compute_local_fun(): numeric vector length x. compute_outliers(): list() vectors length x numeric local medians logical identifying is_outlier. compute_valid_neighbours(): list length NA values x numeric vectors sample indices length width samples span units time t valid values neighbouring split either side invalid NAs.

title: Computes rolling local values — compute_local_windows
text: local rolling window can specified either width number samples, span timespan units t. Specifying width often faster span. align defaults "centre" local window around idx [idx - floor((width-1)/2), idx + floor(width/2)] width specified. Even width values bias align "left", unequal sample forward idx, effectively returning NA last sample index. span specified, local window [t - span/2, t + span/2].

title: Extract intervals from mnirs data — extract_intervals
text: data data frame class "mnirs" containing time series data metadata. nirs_channels character vector list() character vectors mNIRS channel names operate within interval (see Details). Names must match column names data exactly. Must specified event_groups contains "ensemble"- averaged intervals. event_groups = "distinct" channel processing occurs. NULL (default), channels retrieved "mnirs" metadata. time_channel character string giving name time sample column. Must match column name data exactly. NULL (default), time_channel metadata attribute data used. event_channel optional character string giving name event/lap column. column may contain character event labels integer lap numbers. Required using by_label() by_lap() start end. Retrieved metadata defined explicitly. sample_rate optional numeric sample rate (Hz) used bin time values ensemble-averaging. NULL, estimated time_channel (see Details). start Specifies intervals begin. Either raw values — numeric time values, character event labels, explicit integer (e.g. 2L) lap numbers — created by_time(), by_label(), by_lap(), by_sample(). end Specifies intervals end. Either raw values — numeric time values, character event labels, explicit integer (e.g. 2L) lap numbers — created by_time(), by_label(), by_lap(), by_sample(). span one- two-element numeric vector c(, ) units time_channel, list() vectors. (default span = c(-60, 60). Applied additively interval boundaries: start end specified: span[1] shifts start times, span[2] shifts end times. start end specified: span[1] span[2] apply window around event). single positive value recycled shift end times (e.g. span = 60 -> c(0, 60)). single negative value recycled shift start times (e.g. span = -60 -> c(-60, 0)). event_groups Either character string list() integer vectors specifying group intervals (see Details). "distinct" default. Extract interval independent data frame. "ensemble" Ensemble-average specified nirs_channel across detected intervals, returning single data frame. list(c(1, 2), c(3, 4)) Ensemble-average specified nirs_channel within group return one data frame per group. zero_time Logical. Default FALSE. TRUE, re-calculates numeric time_channel values start zero within interval data frame. verbose Logical. Default TRUE. display silence (FALSE) warnings information messages helpful troubleshooting. global default can set via options(mnirs.verbose = FALSE).

title: Extract intervals from mnirs data — extract_intervals
text: Interval boundaries specified using helper functions, passing raw values directly: by_time() numeric Time values units time_channel. by_label() character Strings match event_channel. matching occurrences returned. by_lap() explicit integer (e.g. 2L) Lap numbers match event_channel. Resolves first sample lap start, last sample end, samples lap one either start end specified. by_sample() Integer sample indices (row numbers). Raw values supplied start/end auto-coerced: Numeric → by_time() Character → by_label(), Explicit integer (e.g. 2L) → by_lap(). Use by_sample() explicitly sample indices. start end can use different specification types (e.g., start label, end time). lengths differ, shorter recycled.

title: Extract intervals from mnirs data — extract_intervals
text: span applies additive time shift interval boundaries. single numeric value recycled: span = 60 becomes c(0, 60) span = -60 becomes c(-60, 0). start + end: span[1] shifts starts, span[2] shifts ends. example, start = by_time(30), end = by_time(60), span = c(-5, 10) gives interval [25, 70]. start end : span values apply single boundary, like window around event. example, start = by_time(30), span = c(-5, 60) gives [25, 90].

code: ## read example data data <- read_mnirs(     example_mnirs("train.red"),     nirs_channels = c(         smo2_left = "SmO2 unfiltered",         smo2_right = "SmO2 unfiltered"     ),     time_channel = c(time = "Timestamp (seconds passed)"),     zero_time = TRUE,     verbose = FALSE ) |>     ## avoid issues ensemble-averaging irregular samples     resample_mnirs(method = "linear", verbose = FALSE)   ## ensemble-average across multiple intervals interval_list <- extract_intervals(     data,                       ## channels recycled to all intervals by default     nirs_channels = c(smo2_left, smo2_right),     start = by_time(368, 1084), ## manually identified interval start times     span = c(-20, 90),          ## include the last 180-sec of each interval (recycled)     event_groups = "ensemble",  ## ensemble-average across two intervals     zero_time = TRUE            ## re-calculate common time to start from `0` )  interval_list[[1L]] #> # A tibble: 1,101 × 3 #>     time smo2_left smo2_right #>    <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl> #>  1 -20        56.3       59.2 #>  2 -19.9      56.1       59.2 #>  3 -19.8      56.1       59.2 #>  4 -19.7      56.2       58.9 #>  5 -19.6      56.4       58.9 #>  6 -19.5      56.5       58.9 #>  7 -19.4      56.9       58.7 #>  8 -19.3      57.0       58.7 #>  9 -19.2      56.9       59.0 #> 10 -19.1      56.7       58.8 #> # ℹ 1,091 more rows  # \donttest{   if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {     plot(interval_list[[1L]], time_labels = TRUE) +       ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted")   }  # }

Low-pass high-pass filters allow frequencies lower higher cutoff frequency W passed output signal, respectively. Stop-band defines critical range frequencies rejected output signal. code: set.seed(13) sin <- sin(2 * pi * 1:150 / 50) * 20 + 40 noise <- rnorm(150, mean = 0, sd = 6) noisy_sin <- sin + noise without_edge_detection <- filter_butter(     x = noisy_sin,     order = 2,     W = 0.1,     edges = "none" ) with_edge_detection <- filter_butter(     x = noisy_sin,     order = 2,     W = 0.1,     edges = "rep1" )  ggplot2::ggplot(data.frame(), ggplot2::aes(x = seq_along(noise))) +     theme_mnirs() +     scale_colour_mnirs(name = NULL) +     ggplot2::geom_line(ggplot2::aes(y = noisy_sin)) +     ggplot2::geom_line(         ggplot2::aes(y = without_edge_detection, colour = "without_edge_detection")     ) +     ggplot2::geom_line(         ggplot2::aes(y = with_edge_detection, colour = "with_edge_detection")     )

code: x <- c(1, 3, 2, 5, 4, 6, 5, 7) t <- c(0, 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, 7, 10)  ## irregular time with gaps  ## width: centred window of 3 samples filter_ma(x, width = 3) #> [1]       NA 2.000000 3.333333 3.666667 5.000000 5.000000 6.000000       NA  ## partial = TRUE fills edge values with a narrower window filter_ma(x, width = 3, partial = TRUE) #> [1] 2.000000 2.000000 3.333333 3.666667 5.000000 5.000000 6.000000 6.000000  ## span: centred window of 2 time-units (accounts for irregular sampling) filter_ma(x, t, span = 2) #> [1] 2.0 2.0 2.5 4.5 5.0 5.0 5.5 7.0  ## na.rm = FALSE (default): any NA in the window propagates to the result x_na <- c(1, NA, 3, 4, 5, NA, 7, 8) filter_ma(x_na, width = 3, na.rm = FALSE) #> Warning: ! `x` contains internal NA's. #> ℹ Set `na.rm = TRUE` to ignore NA's. #> [1] NA NA NA  4 NA NA NA NA  ## na.rm = TRUE: skip NAs and return the local mean of local valid values filter_ma(x_na, width = 3, partial = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE) #> [1] 1.0 2.0 3.5 4.0 4.5 6.0 7.5 7.5

title: Replace outliers, invalid, and missing values in mnirs data — replace_mnirs

NULL (default), nirs_channels metadata attribute data used. time_channel character string giving name time sample column. Must match column name data exactly. NULL (default), time_channel metadata attribute data used. invalid_values numeric vector invalid values replaced, e.g. invalid_values = c(0, 100, 102.3). Default NULL replace invalid values. invalid_above, invalid_below Numeric values specifying cutoff values, (respectively) replaced, inclusive specified cutoff values. outlier_cutoff numeric value local outlier threshold, number standard deviations local median. Default NULL replace outliers. Lower values sensitive flag outliers; higher values conservative. outlier_cutoff = 3 Pearson's 3 sigma edit rule. outlier_cutoff = 2 approximates Tukey-style 1.5×IQR rule. outlier_cutoff = 0 Tukey's median filter. width integer defining local window number samples centred idx, [idx - floor(width/2), idx + floor(width/2)]. span numeric value defining local window timespan around idx units time_channel t, [t - span/2, t + span/2]. method character string indicating handle NA replacement (see Details): "linear" Replaces NAs via linear interpolation (default) using stats::approx(). "median" Replaces NAs local median valid values within centred window defined width span. "locf" "Last observation carried forward". Replaces NAs recent valid value left trailing NAs right leading NAs, using stats::approx(). "none" Returns NAs without replacement. verbose Logical. Default TRUE. display silence (FALSE) warnings information messages helpful troubleshooting. global default can set via options(mnirs.verbose = FALSE). x numeric vector response variable. t optional numeric vector predictor variable (e.g. time). Default seq_along(x). ... Additional arguments.

code: ## vectorised operations x <- c(1, 999, 3, 4, 999, 6) replace_invalid(x, invalid_values = 999, width = 3, method = "median") #> [1] 1 2 3 4 5 6  (x_na <- replace_outliers(x, outlier_cutoff = 3, width = 3, method = "none")) #> [1]  1 NA  3  4 NA  6  replace_missing(x_na, method = "linear") #> [1] 1 2 3 4 5 6  ## read example data data <- read_mnirs(     file_path = example_mnirs("moxy_ramp"),     nirs_channels = c(smo2 = "SmO2 Live"),     time_channel = c(time = "hh:mm:ss"),     verbose = FALSE )  ## clean data data_clean <- replace_mnirs(     data,                  ## channels retrieved from metadata     invalid_values = 0,    ## known invalid values in the data     invalid_above = 90,    ## remove data spikes above 90     outlier_cutoff = 3,    ## Pearson's 3 sigma edit rule     width = 10,            ## window for outlier detection and interpolation     method = "linear"      ## linear interpolation over NAs )  # \donttest{   if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {     ## plot original and show where values have been replaced     ## ignore warning about replacing the existing colour scale     plot(data, time_labels = TRUE) +       ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(         name = NULL,         breaks = c("smo2", "replaced"),         values = palette_mnirs(2)       ) +       ggplot2::geom_point(         data = data[data_clean$smo2 != data$smo2, ],         ggplot2::aes(y = smo2, colour = "replaced"),         na.rm = TRUE       ) +       ggplot2::geom_line(         data = {           data_clean[!is.na(data$smo2), "smo2"] <- NA           data_clean         },         ggplot2::aes(y = smo2, colour = "replaced"),         linewidth = 1, na.rm = TRUE       )   } #> Scale for colour is already present. #> Adding another scale for colour, which will replace the existing scale.  # }

"locf" ("Last observation carried forward"). Fills new missing samples recent valid non-NA value left, nearest valid value right leading NAs. Safe numeric, integer, character columns. "linear" Fills new missing samples via linear interpolation using stats::approx(). Suitable numeric columns ; non-numeric columns fall back "locf" behaviour. verbose Logical. Default TRUE. display silence (FALSE) warnings information messages helpful troubleshooting. global default can set via options(mnirs.verbose = FALSE).

title: mnirs 0.6.2
text: read_mnirs(): Now properly parses time_channel fractional unix values; .e. timestamp (e.g. “hh:mm:ss”) values saved Excel infinite wisdom numeric fractional Unix timestamps. now properly coerced numeric POSIXct timestamp values can returned. Timestamps now returned user’s local time zone.

title: mnirs 0.6.2
text: resample_mnirs(): Update default method = "none". Less opinionated default force users explicitly opt-specifying either “linear” “locf” methods fill/interpolate across new samples. Updated package documentation. replace_invalid(), replace_outliers(), replace_missing(): Remove bypass_checks arg intended internal use , bypass redundant checks calling replace_mnirs().

title: mnirs 0.6.2
text: plot.mnirs(): longer coerces long format data behind scenes. plot.mnirs(): y-axis title changed “signal” “mNIRS”. Create AGENTS.md LLM-friendly instructions. Added .Rbuildignore better packaging solution found. Fix lap marker inconsistency train.red_intervals.csv. Updated relevant interval times documentation. README.md & “reading-mnirs-data.qmd” vignette updates. Update recommended core processing sequence: read_mnirs() -> resample_mnirs() -> replace_mnirs() -> … Update extract_intervals() vignette train.red_intervals.csv end-interval reoxygenation events. Small documentation changes.

title: mnirs 0.6.1
text: Core functions updated accept nirs_channel args list. info message displayed list required, instead erroring. Additional info messages displayed shift_mnirs(), rescale_mnirs(), extract_intervals() nirs_channels specified list, retrieved metadata. reminder grouping requirements functions. Core functions now properly update nirs_channels metadata re-specified. Previously, specifying nirs_channels function add additional column name strings existing metadata rather overwrite . Meaning nirs_channels removed metadata using create_mnirs_data(nirs_channels = "..."). updated behaviour mean channels need re-specified less often. filter_mnirs(): Fixed error matching method arguments left blank. README “Reading Cleaning Data {mnirs}” vignette updated fix typos small edits.

title: mnirs 0.6.0
text: NEWS.md truncated recent relevant release updates. full NEWS.md remains dev branch. development functions omitted package build expectation CRAN review. still present dev branch can installed pak::pak("jemarnold/mnirs").

title: mnirs 0.6.0
text: filter_mnirs(): Method-specific arguments (e.g. order method = "butterworth", width method = "moving_average") removed generic function call. Continue passed appropriate method via .... Documentation info/warning/abort messages updated. filter_ma() better separates effects partial na.rm args: partial = FALSE default returns NA edges insufficient number samples available compared specified width span. partial = TRUE calculates mean values edges, long one valid non-NA sample available. na.rm = FALSE default behaves expected mean(na.rm = FALSE), propagating NAs local window calculated mean warning. NOTE differs behaviour na.rm = FALSE filter_butter(), errors internal NAs present. changed. na.rm = TRUE ignores NAs calculates local means long one valid sample present. plot.mnirs() Add new arg: points = TRUE plot points addition lines, useful quick shortcut. Update na.omit now omits non-valid c(NA, NaN, Inf, -Inf) values plotting, just NA. palette_mnirs() can now accept either single numeric value specifying number colours return, number (valid) character colour names.

title: mnirs 0.5.2
text: Update online shiny app hosted https://jemarnold-mnirs-app.share.connect.posit.cloud/ basic reading pre-processing functionality.

title: mnirs 0.5.1
text: read_mnirs() now better automated channel detection logic “Artinis” / “Oxysoft” file formats. nirs_channels time_channel can left blank automatically “sample”, “time” (sample_rate), nirs channel “2”. replace_outliers() now accepts numeric outlier_cutoff values precise outlier detection thresholds (previously integers ). Documentation also updated.

title: mnirs 0.5.0
text: read_mnirs() expands event_channel work integer “lap” numbers, character event label previous. now work .csv file formats; previously read errors may occured file contained header rows data table, resulting improper detection columns. event_channel can now specified integer lap column, addition character column previous. {mnirs} functions may expect event_channel either character integer-ish. extract_intervals(): Function arguments start end used specify one start end point target interval. Specify start/end values helper functions by_time(), by_label(), by_lap(), by_sample(). Numeric values automatically coerced “time” values; Explicit integer values (e.g. 2L) coerced “lap”; Character strings coerced event “label”.
